methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $300M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $350M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

data.narrative:
GrayMatter Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ariyan Kabir and Brual Shah, currently valued at $300M+ on $70M+ raised. The peer universe comprises five private robotics companies at Series B stage, with valuations ranging from $350M to $10.0B, alongside one public comparable.

Two valuation methods triangulate a fair value range. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier to the $300M last post-money valuation, implying $570M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-stage robotics comparables yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range of $350M to $10.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects segmentation within frontier robotics—early-stage humanoid and general-purpose platforms (1X Technologies at $10.0B) command significant premiums over task-specific automation plays.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $484M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The stage-progression method anchors the conservative end near GrayMatter's current $300M post-money. The peer-median method substantially exceeds this, suggesting either market maturation in the sector or execution-dependent upside dependent on near-term milestones. Key uncertainties include GrayMatter's product commercialization timeline, competitive positioning within the peer set, and capital intensity relative to peers at this stage.
